openmeter/entitlement.purpose: Feature entitlement management across three sub-types (metered, boolean, static) with scheduling, override, and supersede lifecycle operations. The metered sub-type drives credit grant burn-down via the credit engine and ClickHouse usage queries; the service layer composes all sub-types behind a single entitlement.Service interface.

patterns.name: Sub-type connector dispatch via Parser
patterns.description: entitlement.Service dispatches to the correct sub-type connector (metered, boolean, static) using the sub-type discriminator on Entitlement.EntitlementType. The Parser singleton in driver/parser.go converts between wire types and domain types.
patterns.example: // driver/parser.go: Parser.ToAPIGeneric dispatches on EntitlementType to call the correct sub-type serializer

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[Entitlement] fan-out
patterns.description: entitlement.Service embeds models.ServiceHooks[Entitlement]. Other packages register hooks via Service.RegisterHooks() to react to entitlement lifecycle without creating circular imports.
patterns.example: // service/service.go: s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, entitlement) after creation

patterns.name: transaction.Run for composite mutations
patterns.description: All operations touching multiple tables (ResetEntitlementUsage, CreateEntitlement with grants, DeleteEntitlement with cleanup) run inside transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue to ensure atomicity.
patterns.example: // metered/reset.go: transaction.Run wraps EndCurrentUsagePeriod + balance snapshot + event publish

patterns.name: Distributed lock before per-customer operations
patterns.description: entitlement.Service acquires a pg_advisory_xact_lock via lockr.Locker before operations that modify multiple entitlement rows for the same customer, preventing race conditions in concurrent reset/grant flows.
patterns.example: // service/lock.go: s.locker.LockForTX(ctx, customerID) — always inside an active transaction

patterns.name: MeasureUsageFromInput wrapper — never set ts directly
patterns.description: MeasureUsageFromInput is a value wrapper (not a plain time.Time) to distinguish NOW vs CURRENT_PERIOD_START enums. Always use FromTime or FromEnum; never assign the ts field directly.
patterns.example: mu := &MeasureUsageFromInput{}; mu.FromEnum(MeasureUsageFromCurrentPeriodStart, currPeriod, now)

patterns.name: EntitlementResetEventV3 from new code only
patterns.description: After any balance change (reset, grant, delete), the metered connector publishes a snapshot event. New code must use EntitlementResetEventV3 from entitlement/metered/events.go; never emit the deprecated v1 variant.
patterns.example: // metered/events.go: NewEntitlementResetEventV3 constructor — never entitlement.SnapshotEvent literal

patterns.name: ValidateUniqueConstraint before scheduled entitlement creation
patterns.description: When scheduling or superseding entitlements, ValidateUniqueConstraint(ents) must be called to ensure no two entitlements for the same feature+customer overlap in their active cadence.
patterns.example: // uniqueness.go: ValidateUniqueConstraint builds a SortedCadenceList and returns UniquenessConstraintError on overlap

key_file_guides.role: entitlement.Service interface with all 13 lifecycle methods plus models.ServiceHooks[Entitlement] embed. Also defines ListEntitlementsParams.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetEntitlementOfCustomerAt resolves by ID first, then by FeatureKey+CustomerID — never pass raw subject keys; always resolve to customer.ID first.

key_file_guides.role: Entitlement domain struct (GenericProperties + sub-type fields), CreateEntitlementInputs, and all validation logic including UsagePeriod minimum 1-hour check.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeasureUsageFromInput is a wrapper type — FromTime / FromEnum are the only valid constructors. ActiveTo before ActiveFrom returns a Validate() error.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementRepo interface — the persistence boundary. Intentionally separate from entitlement.Service so validators can depend on it without importing the full service graph.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntitlementRepo embeds entutils.TxCreator; adapters must implement the TxUser+TxCreator triad.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors (AlreadyExistsError, NotFoundError, WrongTypeError, InvalidValueError, ForbiddenError). AlreadyExistsError wraps GenericConflictError (409).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use typed error constructors — plain fmt.Errorf falls through to 500 in the HTTP encoder.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementCreatedEventV2 and EntitlementDeletedEventV2 CloudEvent types with pinned v2 versions and NewEntitlement*EventPayloadV2 constructors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: mapEntitlementToV2Literal must be used for all new event construction — never build entitlementEventV2EntitlementLiteral with a struct literal.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateUniqueConstraint and UniquenessConstraintError — validates no two entitlements for the same feature+customer overlap in their active cadence.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Relies on models.NewSortedCadenceList; entitlements where ActiveFromTime == ActiveToTime are filtered out before overlap detection.

anti_patterns: Calling sub-type connector methods directly from HTTP handlers — always go through entitlement.Service.
Constructing CreateEntitlementInputs with ActiveTo set but ActiveFrom not set — Validate() returns an error.
Adding DB queries directly to entitlement.Service struct — all persistence goes through EntitlementRepo.
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in service or adapter methods — breaks test determinism with frozen clocks.
Emitting EntitlementResetEvent v1 from new code — use EntitlementResetEventV3 from entitlement/metered/events.go.

decisions.decision: Three independent sub-type connectors (metered, boolean, static) composed behind a single entitlement.Service orchestrator.
decisions.rationale: Sub-types have radically different value semantics (credit burn-down vs on/off vs JSON config); keeping them separate prevents the service layer from accumulating type switches.

decisions.decision: Balance worker subscribes to three topics (system, ingest, balance-worker) via a RecalculateEvent intermediary on the balance-worker topic.
decisions.rationale: The two-stage approach (lifecycle event → RecalculateEvent → snapshot) enables deduplication and filter-before-recalculate discipline, preventing redundant ClickHouse queries on burst events.

decisions.decision: EntitlementRepo is kept separate from entitlement.Service interface.
decisions.rationale: Allows entitlement/validators/customer to depend on the lighter EntitlementRepo without pulling in the full service graph, avoiding circular imports.

code_examples.scenario: Creating a metered entitlement with initial grant

openmeter/notification.purpose: Domain contract layer for the notification subsystem: defines all types (Channel, Rule, Event, EventPayload union, delivery status), composite Service interface, and EventHandler lifecycle interface. Sub-packages (adapter, service, httpdriver, consumer, eventhandler, webhook) import this root for shared vocabulary — no implementation lives here.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions on every Input type
patterns.description: Every Input type declares `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)` and `var _ models.CustomValidator[XInput] = (*XInput)(nil)` to guarantee Validate() and ValidateWith() are implemented.

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError wrapping errors.Join
patterns.description: All Validate() methods accumulate errors into a []error slice and return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — never return raw errors or construct error types directly.

patterns.name: Union config/payload types with type-discriminated Validate()
patterns.description: ChannelConfig, RuleConfig, and EventPayload are union structs with a *Meta type field; Validate() switches on the discriminator and delegates to the sub-type validator. Adding a new event/channel type requires updating both the union struct and its Validate() switch.

patterns.name: eventTypes slice as the single registry for valid EventType values
patterns.description: EventType.Validate() uses lo.Contains(eventTypes, t). Adding a new EventType requires appending to the eventTypes package-level var in event.go — not just adding a const.

patterns.name: EventPayloadVersionCurrent = 1 on all new event payloads
patterns.description: EventPayloadMeta.Version must be set to EventPayloadVersionCurrent (1) when constructing payloads for new events. The adapter reads this version to apply migration logic for older payloads.
patterns.example: EventPayloadMeta{Type: EventTypeInvoiceCreated, Version: EventPayloadVersionCurrent}

patterns.name: MaxChannelsPerRule constant enforced in Input.Validate()
patterns.description: CreateRuleInput and UpdateRuleInput check len(Channels) <= MaxChannelsPerRule (5) inside Validate() — never inline the constant.
patterns.example: if len(i.Channels) > MaxChannelsPerRule { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(...)) }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface as composition of ChannelService, RuleService, EventService, and FeatureService. All HTTP handlers and consumers depend on notification.Service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods directly to Service instead of a sub-interface breaks the smallest-surface dependency principle.

key_file_guides.role: EventPayload union type + EventPayloadVersionCurrent constant. eventPayload.Validate() is the entry-point for payload correctness checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New event type added to EventPayload struct without updating Validate() switch — payload passes validation silently.

key_file_guides.role: EventHandler interface (EventDispatcher + EventReconciler + Start/Close). Defines DefaultReconcileInterval, DefaultDispatchTimeout, and default timeout constants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dispatch is a fire-and-forget contract — implementations must not block inside Dispatch.

key_file_guides.role: eventTypes registry, EventType, Event struct, all *EventInput types. Contains the canonical list of valid event types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New EventType const without appending to eventTypes slice — EventType.Validate() will reject it.

key_file_guides.role: Channel and ChannelConfig union type with discriminated Validate(). WebHookChannelConfig validated separately.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new channel type without handling it in ChannelConfig.Validate() switch causes silent validation bypass.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface (ChannelRepository + RuleRepository + EventRepository + entutils.TxCreator) — the persistence contract implemented by the adapter sub-package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never import the adapter sub-package from this root package — causes import cycles.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic (Svix calls, DB queries) to this root package — it is a types+interfaces-only package.
Defining a new EventType const without adding it to the eventTypes slice in event.go — breaks EventType.Validate().
Returning raw errors from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(...)) — breaks HTTP 400 mapping.
Adding a new payload variant to EventPayload struct without updating EventPayload.Validate() switch — new payloads silently bypass validation.
Importing openmeter/notification/adapter, /service, or /webhook/svix from this package — creates import cycles.

decisions.decision: Root package contains only types, interfaces, and constants — no implementations.
decisions.rationale: All sub-packages import this root for shared types; any implementation here would create import cycles between adapter, service, and consumer sub-packages.

decisions.decision: EventPayloadVersionCurrent is a single int constant rather than per-event versioning.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies migration logic in the adapter — it only needs to branch on version for event types whose schema changed (currently invoice).

decisions.decision: EventHandler is composed of EventDispatcher and EventReconciler sub-interfaces.
decisions.rationale: Allows callers that only need to dispatch (not reconcile) to depend on the smaller EventDispatcher interface.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new event type: extend eventTypes registry and union payload type

openmeter/sink.purpose: High-throughput Kafka-to-ClickHouse sink worker: consumes raw CloudEvent messages from Kafka partitions, deduplicates via Redis or in-memory, batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, then fires post-flush callbacks so the balance-worker can recalculate entitlements. Primary constraint: exactly-once guarantee requires strict flush ordering (ClickHouse insert → Kafka offset commit → Redis dedupe).

patterns.name: Three-phase flush ordering: storage → offset commit → Redis dedupe
patterns.description: flush() always writes ClickHouse first (persistToStorage), then stores Kafka offsets (Consumer.StoreMessage), then sets Redis dedupe keys. Reversing this order breaks the exactly-once guarantee on consumer restart.
patterns.example:
// sink.go: 1. persistToStorage → 2. Consumer.StoreMessage → 3. dedupeSet
// Never reorder these three phases.

patterns.name: FlushEventHandler called in a goroutine after flush, never blocking
patterns.description: OnFlushSuccess is dispatched in a separate goroutine with a FlushSuccessTimeout-bounded context. Never call FlushEventHandler synchronously from flush() — it blocks the main sink loop and causes backpressure on Kafka partitions.

patterns.name: SinkConfig.Validate() before any Sink construction
patterns.description: All Sink configuration is validated in SinkConfig.Validate() before NewSink returns. Every required field has an explicit nil/zero check; callers must not pass partially-configured SinkConfig.

patterns.name: NamespacedMeterCache for meter definitions — never query meter.Service in the hot path
patterns.description: Meter definitions are cached in NamespacedMeterCache and periodically re-fetched at MeterRefetchInterval. Access meter definitions via meterCache.GetMetersByType(), never query meter.Service directly in the Kafka consume path.
patterns.example: meters, err := s.meterCache.GetMetersByType(message)

patterns.name: Storage interface injected via SinkConfig — never instantiated inside sink.go
patterns.description: ClickHouseStorage implements Storage. Tests inject a mock. Never instantiate ClickHouseStorage directly inside sink.go.
patterns.example: type Storage interface { BatchInsert(ctx context.Context, messages []sinkmodels.SinkMessage) error }

patterns.name: SinkBuffer.RemoveByPartitions on Kafka partition revocation
patterns.description: On rebalance, SinkBuffer.RemoveByPartitions(revokedPartitions) must be called before the rebalance completes to drop buffered messages for revoked partitions and prevent double processing.
patterns.example: buffer.RemoveByPartitions(revokedPartitions)

key_file_guides.role: Main Sink struct, SinkConfig, NewSink constructor, flush() orchestration, Run() event loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The flush mutex (s.mu) serialises partition pause/resume with close — any change to flush ordering risks exactly-once violation; the mu.Lock must cover the full ClickHouse+offset+Redis sequence.

key_file_guides.role: Storage interface and ClickHouseStorage implementation. Reads ingested_at Kafka header; generates a ULID StoreRowID per event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ingested_at header parse failure returns an error stopping the entire batch — validate header format at ingest time.

key_file_guides.role: SinkBuffer: mutex-guarded map keyed by TopicPartition for deduplication within a batch. Add is idempotent on same TopicPartition+Offset.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RemoveByPartitions used on partition revocation — callers must call this before rebalance completes.

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedMeterCache: RWMutex-guarded namespace→MetersByType map with atomic isRunning flag. Periodic refresh goroutine started by Start().
key_file_guides.watch_for: isRunning must be set before starting the ticker goroutine; double-start is a no-op but the cache will not refresh.

key_file_guides.role: SinkMessage (pipeline carrier), ProcessingStatus/ProcessingState (OK/DROP). Pure types package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here; importing streaming or meter from models creates a circular dependency.

key_file_guides.role: FlushEventHandler interface + buffered async impl with two-phase shutdown (stopChan signal then drain loop).
key_file_guides.watch_for: WaitForDrain() must be called before process exit or buffered messages will be dropped.

anti_patterns: Changing flush() to write Redis dedupe before Kafka offset commit — violates exactly-once guarantee on consumer restart.
Calling FlushEventHandler.OnFlushSuccess synchronously inside flush() — blocks the main sink loop and causes backpressure on Kafka partitions.
Querying meter.Service directly inside the hot Kafka consume path instead of NamespacedMeterCache — causes per-message DB round trips.
Constructing ClickHouseStorage inside sink.go instead of injecting via SinkConfig.Storage — breaks testability.
Adding new ProcessingState iota values without implementing ProcessingState.String() and handling them in persistToStorage() — results in an 'unknown state type' error at runtime.

decisions.decision: Flush is protected by a mutex that also covers partition pause/resume.
decisions.rationale: Prevents the sink from closing mid-flush when a rebalance arrives; ensures ClickHouse write + offset commit + Redis dedupe are atomic from the consumer's perspective.

decisions.decision: Storage is an interface injected via SinkConfig, not instantiated inside Sink.
decisions.rationale: Allows test doubles for ClickHouse without spinning up a real ClickHouse instance; aligns with the layered adapter pattern used across the codebase.

decisions.decision: FlushEventHandler dispatched in a goroutine with a FlushSuccessTimeout.
decisions.rationale: Post-flush notification (for balance recalculation) must not block the main ingest path; a timeout bounds the goroutine lifetime even if downstream is slow.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new FlushEventHandler for post-flush side effects

openmeter/watermill.purpose: Kafka-backed pub-sub abstraction layer for all async domain event passing between OpenMeter binaries. Owns three named topics (ingest, system, balance-worker), topic routing by event-name prefix, CloudEvents 1.0 wire format, standard Watermill router middleware stack, and driver selection (kafka/noop). All domain event producers and consumers depend on sub-packages here — never on raw Watermill or confluent-kafka-go primitives.

patterns.name: Always publish via eventbus.Publisher — never to a Kafka topic string
patterns.description: Topic routing (ingest vs system vs balance-worker) is determined by event-name prefix inside eventbus.GeneratePublishTopic. Publishing directly to a Kafka topic bypasses this routing and breaks consumer group isolation.
patterns.example: publisher.Publish(ctx, &billingevents.InvoiceCreated{...}) // routes automatically by event-name prefix

patterns.name: All workers use router.NewDefaultRouter as their router factory
patterns.description: NewDefaultRouter wires the fixed middleware stack (PoisonQueue, DLQ telemetry, CorrelationID, Recoverer, exponential-backoff Retry, ProcessingTimeout+RestoreContext, HandlerMetrics). Never construct *message.Router directly.
patterns.example: router, err := router.NewDefaultRouter(router.Options{Tracer: t, MetricMeter: m, ...})

patterns.name: Unknown event types silently dropped via NoPublishingHandler
patterns.description: grouphandler.NoPublishingHandler dispatches to registered GroupEventHandlers keyed by CloudEvents ce_type header. Unregistered event types are dropped (not errored) to support rolling deploys — never return errors for unknown event types.

patterns.name: EventVersionSubsystem prefix determines topic routing
patterns.description: eventbus.GeneratePublishTopic matches event name prefixes against subsystem constants. New events must have EventName() starting with a recognised subsystem prefix; otherwise they silently route to SystemEventsTopic.

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 wire format — Event interface with EventName, EventMetadata, Validate
patterns.description: All events must implement marshaler.Event (EventName, EventMetadata, Validate). Marshal sets ce_type from EventName(); never set CloudEventsHeaderType manually in message metadata. Validate() is called on both producer and consumer sides.

patterns.name: MaxRetries off-by-one: 0 means one attempt then DLQ
patterns.description: router.Options.MaxRetries=0 means zero retries (one attempt total, then DLQ). Use MaxRetries=1 for one attempt plus one retry. Never set MaxRetries=0 intending 'no retries with no DLQ' — the message will still go to DLQ on failure.
patterns.example:
// MaxRetries=0 → 1 attempt → DLQ on failure
// MaxRetries=1 → 1 attempt + 1 retry → DLQ on second failure

key_file_guides.role: Publisher interface and concrete EventBus with topic routing by event-name prefix. Single injection point for all event producers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Event names without a registered subsystem prefix silently route to SystemEventsTopic — verify prefix mapping when adding new event families.

key_file_guides.role: NoPublishingHandler multiplexer. Unknown event types are dropped. AddHandler is safe to call after construction (RWMutex-guarded).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never return errors for unknown event types — causes infinite Watermill retries and DLQ poisoning.

key_file_guides.role: CloudEvents 1.0 serializer/deserializer. Calls Validate() on deserialized events. ULID auto-ID if ID is empty; time.Now() if Time is zero.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() implementations that are too strict can reject valid events from older producers during rolling deploys.

key_file_guides.role: NewDefaultRouter: fixed middleware order. MaxRetries off-by-one: config value 0 = 1 attempt, 1 = 2 attempts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add Timeout middleware without RestoreContext immediately before it (watermill#467 workaround) — cancelled contexts break retry logic.

key_file_guides.role: Organizational parent for kafka/ (IBM Sarama-backed) and noop/ driver implementations. Wire selects driver via config flag.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No source files belong directly in driver/ — only in kafka/ or noop/ sub-packages.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter utilities between Watermill's NoPublishHandlerFunc and HandlerFunc.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler panics with ErrMessagesProduced if the adapted handler returns messages — use only when the handler never produces.

anti_patterns: Constructing a *message.Router directly instead of router.NewDefaultRouter — middleware stack (DLQ, OTel, Retry) will be missing.
Defining event names without a stable EventVersionSubsystem prefix — routing silently falls through to SystemEventsTopic.
Adding source files directly in openmeter/watermill/driver/ — all driver code must live in kafka/ or noop/ sub-packages.
Setting MaxRetries=0 in router.Options intending 'no retries' — the subtraction logic results in 1 attempt then DLQ.
Publishing directly to a raw Kafka topic string from domain code — bypasses eventbus routing and breaks consumer group isolation.

decisions.decision: Three fixed Kafka topics with prefix-based routing inside eventbus rather than per-event topic configuration.
decisions.rationale: Topic isolation matches the worker topology (ingest/balance/system); centralising routing in eventbus prevents producers from accidentally writing to the wrong consumer group.

decisions.decision: CloudEvents 1.0 as the wire format for all Watermill messages.
decisions.rationale: Provides a standard schema for ce_type header-based routing in grouphandler and interoperability with Svix webhook payloads.

decisions.decision: Unknown event types are silently dropped in NoPublishingHandler rather than errored.
decisions.rationale: Allows independent deployment of producers and consumers — a consumer can safely ignore event types it has not yet registered without poisoning its DLQ.

code_examples.scenario: Registering a new event handler in a worker's NoPublishingHandler
